Treatise on the Soul and Its Origin
Another of Victor’s Errors, that the Soul is Corporeal.
originalBook: Treatise on the Soul and Its Origin
title: Another of Victor’s Errors, that the Soul is Corporeal.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Chapter 5 [V.]—Another of Victor’s Errors, that the Soul is Corporeal.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: But as regards his contention, “that the soul is not spirit, but body,” what else can he mean to make out, than that we are composed, not of soul and body, but of two or even three bodies?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: For inasmuch as he says that we consist of spirit, soul and body, and asserts that all the three are bodies; it follows, that he supposes us to be made up of three bodies.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: How absurd this conclusion is, I think ought rather to be demonstrated to him than to you.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: But this is not an intolerable error on the part of a person who has not yet discovered that there is in existence a something, which, though it be not corporeal, yet may wear somewhat of the similitude of a body.
